jokes.text: Dad died. / No, no, he's fine. / What do you know? / I'm relieved.
jokes.setup: Lisa dramatically announces their father's death
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual dismissal and Lisa's equally casual relief
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with efficient dialogue, but fairly standard sibling interaction

jokes.text: Everything changes when you hit the big 1-0. Your legs go. Candy doesn't taste as good.
jokes.setup: Lisa excited about turning eight
jokes.punchline: Bart treating age 10 like elderly decline
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character comedy - his premature world-weariness at age 10 is absurd and well-constructed

jokes.text: This old-timer does ramble on sometimes.
jokes.setup: Bart's complaints about aging at 10
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his own rambling
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency and self-awareness, extends the age joke nicely

jokes.text: If you haven't asked your parents' permission-- naughty, naughty... but Krusty forgives you.
jokes.setup: Children's entertainer on premium phone line
jokes.punchline: Krusty encouraging kids to disobey parents for profit
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of exploitative children's entertainment, perfectly in Krusty's character

jokes.text: I tried to stop her, but she overpowered me.
jokes.setup: Marge asks why Bart didn't watch baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: Claiming a baby overpowered a 10-year-old
jokes.explanation: Classic excuse-making comedy with good economy, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: Pink?! I can't wear pink to work. Everybody wears white. I'm not popular enough to be different.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers his shirt turned pink
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of his social status
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - Homer's self-awareness about his unpopularity is both funny and oddly touching

jokes.text: It's all over, Marge! It's all over!
jokes.setup: Homer's distress about the pink shirt
jokes.punchline: Treating laundry mishap as life-ending catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overreaction, good character consistency but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Ah, my lucky red hat. Clean as a whistle. You did this to me!
jokes.setup: Homer praising his clean hat
jokes.punchline: Immediately blaming Bart for causing the pink shirt problem
jokes.explanation: Good structure with the ironic reversal from praise to blame

jokes.text: Why is that man in pink?! That's Homer Simpson-- a boob from Sector 7-G.
jokes.setup: Burns notices Homer in pink on security monitor
jokes.punchline: Smithers' casual, specific identification and insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work - Burns' confusion and Smithers' detailed knowledge of Homer's mediocrity

jokes.text: Judging by his outlandish attire... he's some sort of freethinking anarchist.
jokes.setup: Homer wearing a pink shirt
jokes.punchline: Burns interpreting minor fashion deviation as radical politics
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of conservative paranoia and Burns' disconnect from reality - pink shirt equals anarchist

jokes.text: Yes, these color monitors... have already paid for themselves.
jokes.setup: Burns identifying Homer's pink shirt as suspicious
jokes.punchline: Crediting surveillance technology for catching fashion crimes
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the surveillance paranoia theme with Burns' corporate efficiency mindset

jokes.text: Just because I'm wearing pink... doesn't mean I'm some kind of pink doughnut-eater... although it is tempting.
jokes.setup: Homer defending himself against pink stereotyping
jokes.punchline: Immediately being tempted by the pink donut despite his protest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - making a principled stand then immediately wavering due to food temptation

jokes.text: Spare me the tiresome antics of the Simpson family.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain the laundry situation
jokes.punchline: Doctor dismissing the main characters' hijinks as tiresome
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke acknowledging the show's own comedic formula while advancing the plot

jokes.text: 20 simple questions that will determine... just how crazy or 'meshuggener' someone is.
jokes.setup: Professional psychiatric evaluation
jokes.punchline: Doctor using Yiddish slang casually in medical context
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor with the incongruous Yiddish, but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: All right, all right. I'll get Lisa to do it.
jokes.setup: Marge refusing to fill out Homer's psychological evaluation
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution is to have his 8-year-old daughter do it
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - completely missing the point of a personal psychological assessment

jokes.text: Why, oh, why is my cat dead? Couldn't that Chrysler hit me instead?
jokes.setup: Lisa's sad poem about her dead cat
jokes.punchline: Child wishing she had died in a car accident instead
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and perfectly captures Lisa's dramatic, intellectual nature while being genuinely shocking

jokes.text: No deal. / Dad, you should do this.
jokes.setup: Lisa's dark poetry continuing
jokes.punchline: Homer cutting her off and Lisa's responsible response
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic with Lisa being more responsible than Homer

jokes.text: It's no different... than the time I let you vote for me. Remember that absentee ballot?
jokes.setup: Homer convincing Lisa to fill out his form
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing election fraud as precedent
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer's casual admission of voter fraud as normal parenting

jokes.text: Anyone order a hot dog?
jokes.setup: Watching 'Dog on Fire' video on TV
jokes.punchline: Pun about hot dogs while dog is literally on fire
jokes.explanation: Dark pun with good economy but relies on shock value over clever construction

jokes.text: Do you hear voices? I'm hearing one now, though I'm trying to watch TV.
jokes.setup: Psychological test question about hearing voices
jokes.punchline: Homer interpreting Lisa's voice as the psychiatric symptom
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Homer's literal interpretation of the psychiatric question is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Are you quick to anger? Bart, shut up or I'll shut you up! Yes.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if Homer is quick to anger
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately demonstrating anger, then confirming yes
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic structure - immediate demonstration followed by oblivious confirmation

jokes.text: Do you wet your pants? Even the best of us has an occasional accident.
jokes.setup: Embarrassing psychological test question
jokes.punchline: Homer's dignified but incriminating response
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Homer trying to maintain dignity while admitting embarrassing truth

jokes.text: Careful, men. He wets his pants.
jokes.setup: Orderlies arriving to take Homer away
jokes.punchline: Warning about Homer based on his test answers
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the embarrassment by making it official medical concern

jokes.text: We have a very simple method. Whoever has that stamp on his hand is insane.
jokes.setup: Homer asking how they determine sanity
jokes.punchline: Completely arbitrary stamp-based system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of arbitrary institutional power and mental health system absurdities

jokes.text: I'm a very popular entertainer. Of course, I've heard of you. You'd have to be living under a rock-- What's your name? Michael Jackson. Doesn't ring a bell.
jokes.setup: Michael Jackson claiming to be popular entertainer
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete ignorance despite saying you'd have to live under a rock
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ignorance - using the exact phrase then immediately proving it applies to him

jokes.text: Heard of MTV or Motown? No. Beat It? You beat it. Thriller? What was that?
jokes.setup: Michael Jackson trying increasingly obvious references
jokes.punchline: Homer's consistent cluelessness including mishearing 'Beat It'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's cultural ignorance with the 'Beat It'/'You beat it' mishearing

jokes.text: How do your feet do that? The moonwalk? No, your feet!
jokes.setup: Michael Jackson demonstrating the moonwalk
jokes.punchline: Homer completely missing the famous dance move, focused on basic foot movement
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer obliviousness - completely missing the cultural significance for basic mechanics

jokes.text: I wore a pink shirt. I understand. People thought I dressed crazy. What did you wear? One white glove... covered with rhinestones.
jokes.setup: Homer and Michael bonding over fashion persecution
jokes.punchline: Comparing pink shirt to Jackson's iconic glove as equally crazy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comedy of false equivalence - Homer's minor wardrobe malfunction vs. Jackson's intentional costume choices

jokes.text: Five times nine. Forty-five. Wow! This guy is the chief.
jokes.setup: Introduction to Floyd as idiot savant who can multiply any numbers
jokes.punchline: Homer impressed by basic multiplication any child knows
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's low standards for mathematical achievement

jokes.text: Joe's Crematorium. You kill 'em, we grill 'em.
jokes.setup: Bart answering phone at home
jokes.punchline: Fake business with dark humor and rhyming slogan
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart prank call answer with dark humor and perfect rhyme scheme - very quotable

jokes.text: If you're really Michael Jackson... who were your last four Grammy dates? Brooke Shields, Diana Ross, Emmanuel Lewis and Bubbles.
jokes.setup: Bart testing if it's really Michael Jackson
jokes.punchline: Including his pet chimp Bubbles in list of dates
jokes.explanation: Good reference humor playing on Jackson's real-life relationships and pet chimp

jokes.text: You don't want him to get a lobotomy, do you? Hmm... lobotomy.
jokes.setup: Michael Jackson warning about Homer getting lobotomy
jokes.punchline: Bart considering it might be an improvement
jokes.explanation: Dark family comedy - Bart contemplating brain surgery for his father as potential improvement

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Mother was right.
jokes.setup: Learning Homer is in mental institution
jokes.punchline: Marge realizing her mother's warnings were accurate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment referencing the classic mother-in-law dynamic

jokes.text: I said crazy house! Poorhouse. Crazy house! Poorhouse.
jokes.setup: The twins arguing about what they predicted
jokes.punchline: Petty argument over which institutional prediction was correct
jokes.explanation: Standard twin bickering, fits their characters but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: Hot cakes... football... boobies-- Bubbles, it's going to be a long night.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to think of song topics
jokes.punchline: Escalating to inappropriate content, Michael's patient reaction
jokes.explanation: Homer's limited creativity leading to inappropriate suggestions

jokes.text: Was the door locked?
jokes.setup: Patient describing inability to leave house due to agoraphobia
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation missing the psychological aspect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer literalness in inappropriate therapeutic setting

jokes.text: Was it raining?
jokes.setup: Continuation of patient's psychological problems
jokes.punchline: Homer continuing to look for practical rather than psychological causes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's complete misunderstanding of mental health

jokes.text: Were you out of gas?
jokes.setup: Patient describing long drive to work anxiety
jokes.punchline: Homer's third practical solution attempt
jokes.explanation: Completes the rule of three with Homer's obstinate literal-mindedness

jokes.text: You mean there really is a Bart? Good Lord!
jokes.setup: Doctor learning about Homer's family situation
jokes.punchline: Shock that Bart actually exists rather than being a delusion
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - doctor horrified that Homer's complaints about Bart are real, not symptoms

jokes.text: In 1979, I got real depressed... when my Off the Wall album... just got one lousy Grammy nomination.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he's in mental hospital
jokes.punchline: Depression over 'only' getting Grammy nomination for successful album
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity problems humor - being upset about tremendous success not being even more tremendous

jokes.text: Joe's Taxidermy. You snuff'em, we stuff'em.
jokes.setup: Another phone call to the Simpson house
jokes.punchline: Different fake business with same dark humor pattern
jokes.explanation: Good callback with variation - same format but different business, maintaining Bart's dark humor

jokes.text: I'm a vegetarian, and I don't drink. Are you sure you're here voluntarily?
jokes.setup: Michael Jackson stating he doesn't eat meat or drink alcohol
jokes.punchline: Homer questioning his sanity based on dietary choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - healthy lifestyle choices seem more insane than being in mental hospital

jokes.text: Can you keep a secret? No. Michael Jackson... is coming to my house!
jokes.setup: Bart asking if Milhouse can keep a secret
jokes.punchline: Milhouse honestly saying no, then Bart telling him anyway
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - Milhouse's honesty about being untrustworthy and Bart's impulsiveness

jokes.text: will henceforth be known as the Michael Jackson Expressway.
jokes.setup: Mayor renaming road from Dalai Lama Expressway
jokes.punchline: Immediately changing spiritual leader road to pop star road
jokes.explanation: Escalation of previous joke about Dalai Lama visit

jokes.text: He's 300 pounds. He's white. He's dressed without flair.
jokes.setup: Crowd expecting to see Michael Jackson
jokes.punchline: Perfect description of Homer as the opposite of Jackson
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup describing Homer as anti-Jackson in every way

jokes.text: Don't ever show your face around here again, you tool.
jokes.setup: Crowd disappointed about fake Michael Jackson
jokes.punchline: Personal insult and banishment over celebrity disappointment
jokes.explanation: Standard mob mentality humor, competent but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: Perhaps, if a professional so advises... I will give you a hug... at some far distant family reunion... but rest assured, it will be purely for show.
jokes.setup: Lisa writing formal letter about no longer considering Bart her brother
jokes.punchline: Clinical conditions for potential future affection
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa characterization - overly formal, intelligent, and dramatically precise in her anger

jokes.text: Hey, Looney Tunes, this is what Michael Jackson looks like. You look like a big, fat mental patient.
jokes.setup: Kid showing picture to prove identity
jokes.punchline: Accurate description that reveals the irony - he IS a mental patient
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor - the insult is literally true, he is a mental patient

jokes.text: Although I know you're looking at me, I would look exactly the same, even if you weren't.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting Lisa looks sad because she knows they're watching
jokes.punchline: Lisa's philosophical response about her authentic emotional state
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa intellectualism - overthinking a simple observation with philosophical precision

jokes.text: This is my real voice. I'm Leon Compowski. I'm a bricklayer from New Jersey.
jokes.setup: Character has been Michael Jackson throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Reveal that he's actually a working-class guy from New Jersey
jokes.explanation: Excellent twist ending that recontextualizes the entire episode - great misdirection payoff

jokes.text: All my life I was angry, until one day... I talked like this. All of a sudden... everybody was smiling at me.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining his Michael Jackson voice
jokes.punchline: Simple solution to lifelong anger issues through celebrity impersonation
jokes.explanation: Good character backstory that's both touching and absurd - finding happiness through impersonation

jokes.text: To make a tired point, which one of us is truly crazy?
jokes.setup: Leon's philosophical question about sanity
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment that it's a clichéd question
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor acknowledging the tired 'who's really crazy' trope while using it

jokes.text: Not me. I got this.
jokes.setup: Leon's philosophical question about who's truly crazy
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple logic that his sanity certificate proves he's not crazy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - literal-minded solution to philosophical question with great economy
